prs.title: fix(i18n): replace hardcoded strings in ComparisonDisplay
prs.body:
Fixes #1304

`ComparisonDisplay.svelte` had ~20 hardcoded English strings that bypassed `$_()`, so the comparison page stayed in English regardless of locale.

**Changes:**
- Added 14 new i18n keys under `compare.*` in `en-US.json`
- Replaced all hardcoded strings with `$_()` calls
- Reused existing top-level keys (`nutriscore`, `ecoscore`) where available

The `__check_cache()` function and its six module-level cache variables (`__cache0`, `__cache_X0`, `__cache_f0`, `__cache1`, `__cache_X1`, `__cache_f1`) in `shap/benchmark/metrics.py` are dead code — never called anywhere.

The only reference was a commented-out line:

prs.title: fix: Remove unused $singular variable in Ingredients.pm

labels.name: 🥗🔍 Ingredients analysis
labels.description: https://wiki.openfoodfacts.org/Ingredients_Extraction_and_Analysis

prs.body:
Fixes #13437 

While reviewing `lib/ProductOpener/Ingredients.pm`, I noticed an unused variable `$singular` in two regexp builder functions.

### What changed

Removed the dead `my $singular = ...` assignment from:
- `init_allergens_regexps()` (was at line 314)
- `init_labels_regexps()` (was at line 721)

In both cases, `$singular` was assigned the loop variable's value right before the loop variable was modified in-place with `s/s$//`, but `$singular` was never read after that.

prs.title: Fix/remove debug print and typo in serializable
prs.body:
fix: #4335

Removed a leftover print() that was cluttering stdout on every model load. Also fixed a typo ("wsa" → "was") in a nearby error message.

prs.title: FIX: correct NaN branch routing in GPU TreeSHAP
prs.body:
## Overview
Closes #3936 (partially — addresses the NaN handling bug)

Related to #4182 (GPUTreeExplainer tracker)

## Problem

I was looking into the GPU TreeSHAP issues tracked in #4182 and noticed that `RecurseTree()` in `_cext_gpu.cu` hardcodes `is_missing_branch = false` for both left and right child splits. This means `EvaluateSplit()` always returns `false` for NaN inputs:

## fix 
use children_default[pos] to figure out which child is the default for missing values, then pass that into ShapSplitCondition


## Tests
Added test_gpu_tree_explainer_shap_nan which:
* Takes each model fixture
* Runs both CPU and GPU TreeExplainer on the NaN'd data
* Checks that the SHAP values match within tolerance

prs.title: Add tests for untested utility functions in _general.py
prs.body:
Works towards #3690

Noticed safe_isinstance, ordinal_str, shapley_coefficients, and OpChain.apply had no test coverage. Added tests for each in the existing test_general.py.

prs.title: fix: Parse "* Svensk råvara" as Swedish origin indicator

labels.name: 🧬 Taxonomies
labels.description: https://wiki.openfoodfacts.org/Global_taxonomies

labels.name: 💥 Merge Conflicts
labels.description: 💥 Merge Conflicts

labels.name: 🥗🔍 Ingredients analysis
labels.description: https://wiki.openfoodfacts.org/Ingredients_Extraction_and_Analysis

prs.body:
- Fixes #13258.

Changes:
* Added Svensk råvara to taxonomies/labels.txt with origin en:sweden.
* Added en:swedish-raw-material to @labels in Ingredients.pm so the footnote resolver processes it.
* Added a preparsing test case using the exact ingredient text from the issue.


Why: Swedish products use * Svensk råvara to mark ingredients of Swedish origin. The parser previously treated this footnote as an unrecognized ingredient. This fix leverages the existing symbol-to-label resolution pipeline to automatically set the geographic origin of all starred ingredients to Sweden.

prs.title: feat: Add navigation & discovery event tracking to Matomo
prs.body:
## Description:

Adds Matomo event tracking for navigation and discovery interactions, extending matomo-events.js.

## Events added:

Key page visits — tracks visits to /nutriscore, /nova, /ecoscore, /contribute, /discover
Explore by / Sort by — tracks dropdown button clicks
App store links — tracks Google Play and App Store link clicks
Menu hover — tracks first hover on Tools, User, and Language menus (debounced per session)
Share product — tracks share button clicks

I verified this locally by creating a standalone HTML test page.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dbed3d73-2686-4780-9cca-1050311327ea

Part of #8191

prs.title: feat: add toast notifications for product edit save feedback
prs.body:
Adds user-facing feedback when saving a product edit. Previously, users got no visual indication of whether their save succeeded or failed — it just silently logged to console.

Now the  toast system  shows:

* Success toast — "Product saved successfully!" when the API returns OK
* Error toast — "Failed to save product" if API returns failure, or "An error occurred" if there's a network/unexpected error

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/45355431-7917-46ab-8ac0-a3af7f800333


Part of #125

prs.title: feat: add sticky floating save button to product edit form
prs.body:
## What this PR does 

Adds a **sticky floating save button** that appears in the bottom-right corner of the screen when editing a product. The button intelligently shows/hides based on scroll position — it only appears when the inline save button at the bottom of the form is scrolled out of view.

## Problem 

The product edit form has 6 expandable sections (Languages, Images, Basic Info, Ingredients, Nutrition, Comment), making it extremely long. Previously, the only "Save" button was at the very bottom, forcing users to scroll through the entire form just to save. This is especially painful on mobile devices .



Related to #125


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8e2ab12c-81f3-4082-8b4d-b1613abe96c4

prs.title: fix: exclude obsolete products from CSV/RDF exports
prs.body:
### Discription 
The export_database.pl script was exporting products from both the products and products_obsolete MongoDB collections into the public CSV/RDF dumps. This caused deleted or transferred products (e.g. moved to Open Products Facts) to appear as active with stale data.


### Solution 
This PR removes the products_obsolete collection from the export, so only active products are included in the public dumps

Fixes #13164

prs.title: fix: track Nutri-Score, Eco-Score, and NOVA Matomo events on product

labels.name: Template::Toolkit
labels.description: The templating toolkit used by product opener. The starting point for HTML/JS/CSS fixes.

prs.body:
This PR tracks Matomo events for Nutri-Score, Eco-Score, and NOVA when a user views a product page. 

Since these scores are generated dynamically by Knowledge Panels, I added a small DOM script that waits for the page to load, checks which score images are being displayed, and pushes the exact grades to Matomo.

Fixes part of #8191.

### Events Tracked:
* `has_nutriscore` (e.g,  a, b, c, d, e)
* `has_ecoscore` (e.g,  a, b, c, d, e)
* `has_nova` (e.g,  1, 2, 3, 4)

I tested this locally in the browser by mocking the DOM, and all three events are successfully firing and pushing the correct values to the `_paq` array.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/27f04c1f-1ff8-4b6a-82e6-d317c7034e66

prs.title: fix: mobile Account link navigates to internal profile page instead of Keycloak
prs.body:
### Problem
The mobile **Account** link was navigating to the external Keycloak account management UI (`KEYCLOAK_ACCOUNT_URL`), while the desktop version correctly navigated to the internal `/users/{username}` profile page. This caused an inconsistent user experience across screen sizes.

### Changes
- **Fixed** the mobile Account link in `+layout.svelte` to use `resolve('/users/[user]', { user: $userInfo.preferred_username })` — matching the desktop behavior
- **Removed** the now-unused `KEYCLOAK_ACCOUNT_URL` import

 fix #996

prs.title: fix(layout): correct navigation timeout cleanup
prs.body:
### Problem 
 the 5-second navigationTooSlow timeout used to display the "slow server" dialog was not being cleared on fast navigations
 
### Changes 
 This PR fixes the issue by moving the timeout variable declaration outside the promise and returning the cleanup function directly from the $effect block so it gets properly destroyed when navigation completes.

prs.title: fix: replace unsafe Message.Bytes cast with exhaustive when expression
prs.body:
Fixes #101

The send() method in MqttCourierClient does a hard cast (message as Message.Bytes).value which is unsafe since Message is a sealed class meant for extension. A custom MessageAdapter could return a different subtype, causing a ClassCastException.

Added a toByteArray() method to the Message sealed class using an exhaustive when, and replaced the hard cast with it. This is the same pattern already used in GsonMessageAdapter.fromMessage().

prs.title: fix: restrict map display to food product categories
prs.body:
I have Added a simple guard in display_tag() in Display.pm  that checks two conditions before adding a Wikidata object to the map data:
1) $options{product_type} eq 'food' — maps are only relevant for food products
2) $tagtype eq 'categories' — categories like wine regions or cheese appellations can have geographic boundaries, but brands, ingredients, etc. generally don't

If both conditions aren't met, we just skip adding the wikidata object, so the frontend never makes the SPARQL query and the map section stays hidden.

Fixes #13041

prs.title: fix: Clear password strings from memory after authentication
prs.body:
Implements secure password memory handling by clearing SSH, AWS, and Google Drive credentials immediately after use.

###Changes
Wrapped password usage in try/finally blocks to ensure cleanup even on errors
Clear password variables from memory after authentication
Clear input widget values to prevent retention


###This fixes the three security concerns :
Immediate clearing - Passwords cleared in finally blocks
Minimal scope - Variables only exist within try blocks
Log prevention - Cleared before exceptions can expose them


Fixes #671

prs.title: fix: update deprecated ESLint rules to modern equivalents
prs.body:
Closes #12941

I noticed that `.eslintrc.js` is using some deprecated ESLint rules that have been renamed over the years. This PR updates them to their modern equivalents.

## What Changed

Updated 4 deprecated rules:
- `no-native-reassign` → `no-global-assign`
- `no-negated-in-lhs` → `no-unsafe-negation`  
- `id-blacklist` → `id-denylist`
- Removed `indent-legacy` (no longer needed)

These were deprecated between ESLint v3-v7, so they're 

tty old and should be updated to avoid issues when upgrading ESLint in the future.

## Testing

Ran `npm run lint:js` - everything still works fine, no new errors.

## Why This Matters

- Prevents potential breakage when upgrading to ESLint v9+
- Removes deprecation warnings from console
- The new rule names work exactly the same way, just renamed

prs.title: fix: Prevent Keycloak password overwrite on user preference updates

labels.name: 💥 Merge Conflicts
labels.description: 💥 Merge Conflicts

prs.body:
# Fix for Issue #12936

## Solution
Modified `lib/ProductOpener/Keycloak.pm` to conditionally send credentials to Keycloak.

The changes in `create_or_update_user` now:
1. **Send credentials if `$password` is defined:** (User explicitly sets a new password in PO form) -> Update Keycloak.
2. **Send credentials if user does NOT exist in Keycloak:** (New user creation or first sync) -> Migrate the legacy `encrypted_password`.
3. **Send NO credentials if password field is empty AND user exists:** -> Preserve the existing password in Keycloak.

## Changes
- Updated `create_or_update_user` in `lib/ProductOpener/Keycloak.pm` to handle credential generation logic.
- Updated the `credentials` field in the API request payload to be an empty array `[]` when no credential updates are needed, preventing the overwrite.

prs.title: docs: add macOS VirtioFS guide to fix taxonomy timing issues

labels.name: 📚 Documentation
labels.description: Documentation issues improve the project for everyone.

prs.body:
## What

Added a quick guide for macOS users on enabling VirtioFS in Docker Desktop to prevent "missing taxonomy files" errors during `make dev`.

## Why

On macOS, Docker's default file sharing can be slow, causing timing issues where taxonomy files aren't ready when the backend starts. VirtioFS provides near-native performance and resolves this.

## Changes

- Added new subsection "macOS Users: Use VirtioFS to Avoid File Sync Issues" under Docker Desktop Prerequisite in `docs/dev/how-to-quick-start-guide.md`
- Includes step-by-step instructions for enabling VirtioFS
- Mentions resource recommendations if issues persist

Fixes #12906

prs.title: fix: prevent taxonomy cache growth

labels.name: Template::Toolkit
labels.description: The templating toolkit used by product opener. The starting point for HTML/JS/CSS fixes.

prs.body:
Fixes #12893

This PR prevents the taxonomy cache (build-cache/taxonomies) from growing forever and eventually breaking builds with “no space left on device”.
After writing new taxonomy cache files, we now automatically clean up older ones and keep only the 5 most recent cache sets per taxonomy (grouped by hash). This keeps recent caches available for ongoing PRs/builds while stopping unlimited cache growth.

Tested by validating the cleanup logic and running:

- Confirmed no regressions or infinite re-renders

This PR resolves issue #4617.

issues.title: Bug: ComparisonDisplay.svelte — hardcoded English strings bypass i18n
issues.body:
### Description
`src/lib/ui/ComparisonDisplay.svelte` has ~20 strings written directly in English instead of going through `$_()`. The comparison page stays in English regardless of the user's language setting.

### Affected strings
The following hardcoded strings bypass the translation function:
- Badge labels: `Best`, `Worst`
- Section labels: `Scores:`, `Nutrients / 100g:`, `Fruits/Vegetables/Nuts`
- Desktop table headers: `Name`, `Code (Barcode)`, `Brand`, `Quantity`, `Nutri-Score`, `Nova Group`, `Green-Score`, `N. of additives`, `Nutritional Values (per 100g)`
- Comparison mode hint texts at the bottom of the display

### Proposed fix
- Add new i18n keys under `compare.*` in `en-US.json`
- Reuse existing top-level keys where available (e.g. `nutriscore`, `ecoscore`)
- Replace all hardcoded strings with `$_()` calls

issues.title: Clean up unused `$singular` variable in Ingredients.pm regexp builders
BUG: Debug print statement left in Deserializer.load() + typo in error message
issues.body:
There's a print on line 177 of shap/_serializable.py that looks like it was left in from debugging.

 It prints to stdout every time you load a saved explainer, which clutters the output.
The log.debug() call right above it already does the same thing properly, so the print can just be removed.

Also spotted a small typo on line 170 — "wsa" instead of "was" in an error message.

issues.title: Bug: Incomplete i18n — Only 2 Locales Supported (en-US and it-IT)
issues.body:
## Discription :
Despite being an international database with dozens of languages, openfoodfacts-explorer currently hardcodes only two locales (en-US and it-IT) in src/lib/i18n/index.ts. If a user selects any other language (e.g., French, Spanish, German), the app fails to find a translation file and always falls back to English.

issues.title: Fix strict type errors with environment variables
issues.body:
### Discription
Running pnpm check was failing because SvelteKit types our environment variables (like PUBLIC_ROBOTOFF_URL, PUBLIC_SEARCH_BASE_URL, etc.) as potentially undefined


###  fixed this by
* Adding as string type assertions where we know the keys exist (like Keycloak and Robotoff URLs).
* Updating the check in getSearchBaseUrl() to catch undefined values, not just empty strings.
* Adding a fallback || '' for the Prices API base URL.


Everything now passes type checking with 0 errors

issues.title: Remove 'Chrome only' restriction from Web Share API in Comparison
issues.body:
### Discription 
 In src/routes/compare/+page.svelte the navigator.share() functionality is strictly gated behind an isChromeBrowser user-agent check.

This artificially restricts users on Safari, Firefox Mobile, and other supported browsers from opening their device's native share sheet (e.g., to text or AirDrop comparisons), forcing them to copy to the clipboard instead


### Expected Behavior
Tapping "Share Comparison" opens the OS native share sheet on any capable mobile or desktop browser.

issues.title: Hardcoded lang: 'en' in OAuth login URL ignores user locale
issues.body:
### Description
Currently, when a user initiates the OAuth login flow, the lang parameter passed to the Keycloak API is hardcoded to 'en'. This forces the Keycloak authentication UI to always render in English, completely ignoring the user's actual browser language preferences or the locale currently selected in the application settings


### Expected Behavior: 
The Keycloak login page should render in the language corresponding to the user's current locale in the application or their navigator.language preference. The lang parameter in the OAuth initialization configuration should be dynamic

issues.title: Inverted logic for Robotoff dryRun configuration in +layout.svelte
issues.body:
### Discription 
 There is a logic error in the Robotoff configuration within +layout.svelte. The dryRun property is currently set to !dev. Because of the NOT operator (!), the environment behaviors are completely swapped.

### Expected Behavior
The application should protect the production database during local development (dry-run mode ON) and save real user contributions in production (dry-run mode OFF).

* In Production (dev = false), dryRun should be false.
* In Development (dev = true), dryRun should be true.

### Suggested Fix: 
Remove the ! operator from the configuration

issues.title: Bug: Mobile "Account" link navigates to external Keycloak UI instead of internal profile page
issues.body:
### Current Behavior
* Desktop: Clicking "Account" navigates to /users/{username} (the in-app profile page)
* Mobile: Clicking "Account" navigates to KEYCLOAK_ACCOUNT_URL (external Keycloak account management UI)

### Expected behavior
Both desktop and mobile "Account" links should navigate to the same destination — the internal /users/{username} profile page — to provide a consistent user experience across all screen sizes.

issues.title: Navigation timeout never cleared due to incorrect setTimeout cleanup
issues.body:
### Discription 
In src/routes/+layout.svelte, a 5-second setTimeout is created during navigation to show a "slow server" dialog if the page takes too long to load.
the clearTimeout function is returned from inside the Promise constructor. Since Promise constructors ignore return values, the timeout is never actually cancelled when the page finishes loading and causing phantom "slow server" dialogs on fast views.

### Expected behavior 
The clearTimeout function must be returned to the outer Svelte $effect block so it can be properly destroyed when navigation completes

issues.title: Hardcoded lang="en" in app.html ignores user locale
issues.body:
### What
The root HTML document (src/app.html) has a hardcoded <html lang="en"> tag this hardcoded tag remains English even when the user switches to a different language

### Problem
1) SEO: Search engines get confused when the page content doesn't match the declared HTML language.
2) Screen readers will try to read non-English text with English pronunciation rules

### Expected behavior 
The lang attribute should be dynamically injected by SvelteKit based on the active user locale

issues.title: Search query from global navigation bar is not URL-encoded
issues.body:
### What
When searching for products using the search bar in the global navigation bar (layout), search queries are concatenated directly to the URL without being URL-encoded. This causes searches containing special characters (like &, #, %, +) to fail or return incorrect results.

### Expected 
The query should be encoded using encodeURIComponent
" await goto('/search?q=' + searchQuery); "

issues.title: Bug: Crash in MqttCourierClient.send() with non-Bytes messages
issues.body:
Issue in `MqttCourierClient.send()`.

The code does a hard cast to `Message.Bytes`:

```kotlin
return mqttClient.send(MqttPacket((message as Message.Bytes).value, topic, qos), sendMessageCallback)
```

Since `Message` is a sealed class, it's designed to be extended with new subtypes. The `MessageAdapter.toMessage()` interface also returns `Message` (not `Message.Bytes`), so a custom adapter could return a different subtype in the future, which would crash here with a `ClassCastException`.

Interestingly, the `GsonMessageAdapter.fromMessage()` already handles this correctly using `when (message) { is Message.Bytes -> ... }`. This pattern should be used in `MqttCourierClient.send()` as well for consistency and safety.

**Suggestion:** Add a `toByteArray()` method to the `Message` sealed class with an exhaustive `when`, and use it instead of the hard cast. This way, adding a new `Message` subtype will produce a compile error, preventing silent crashes.

issues.body:

I was analyzing the OIDC authentication flow in `lib/ProductOpener/Auth.pm` and noticed a critical issue in the `start_signout` function.

There appears to be a debug statement leftover in the production code that will cause the application to crash whenever a user signs out with a `return_url` parameter.

issues.body: I noticed that 'max_memory' is documented as 'bool' in several docstrings within 'runners.py' (like in 'run_lems_with_jneuroml' and others) but it should actually be 'str' as it expects values like "400M" or "2G".

issues.title: Cleanup deprecated options from configWhitelist
issues.body:
**Description:**

I was looking through `configWhitelist.ts` and noticed we're still allowing quite a few deprecated configuration options to be passed through. Since we've got newer, better replacements for these (like using `lobby.autoKnock` instead of the old `autoKnockLobby`), keeping the old ones around in the whitelist is sort of just encouraging people to keep using the outdated params.

I think it'd be good to clean this up so we can enforce the new config structure moving forward.

I'm planning to remove these specific options from the whitelist:

*   `autoKnockLobby`
*   `disableDeepLinking`
*   `disableIncomingMessageSound`
*   `disableJoinLeaveSounds`
*   `disableRecordAudioNotification`
*   `disableRemoveRaisedHandOnFocus`
*   `disableSpeakerStatsSearch`
*   `speakerStatsOrder`
*   `enableLobbyChat`
*   `hideAddRoomButton`
*   `hideLobbyButton`
*   `liveStreamingEnabled`
*   `transcribingEnabled`

issues.title: Add @deprecated JSDoc annotations to legacy configuration options
issues.body:
### What happened?



I noticed that while `config.js` has several deprecated options (like `hideLobbyButton`, `transcribingEnabled`, etc.) that are maintained for backward compatibility, the TypeScript definitions in `configType.ts` don't currently mark these fields as deprecated.

This means developers using TypeScript/IDEs don't get any warnings when using deprecated options, making it harder to discover the recommended alternatives (e.g., `securityUi.hideLobbyButton`).

I'd like to propose adding `@deprecated` JSDoc annotations to these fields to improve developer experience and tooling support. 

### Platform

- [ ] Chrome (or Chromium based)
- [ ] Firefox
- [ ] Safari
- [x] Other desktop browser
- [ ] Android browser
- [ ] iOS browser
- [ ] Electron app
- [ ] Android mobile app
- [ ] iOS mobile app
- [ ] Custom app using a mobile SDK

### Browser / app / sdk version

N/A (Documentation/Tooling improvement)

```shell

```

### More details?

_No response_

issues.title: Hardcoded emailVerified => true in Keycloak user creation bypasses verification
issues.body:
It looks like when we're creating or updating a user in Keycloak, we're currently hardcoding the emailVerified flag to true:

    emailVerified => $JSON::PP::true,    # TODO: Keep this for compat with current register endpoint?

I saw the TODO comment there about compatibility, but simply setting it to true for everyone seems a bit risky since it effectively bypasses email verification. Usually, we'd want Keycloak to handle that flow or only set this to true if we've actually verified them on our side.

Is this temporary behavior that we still need, or should we switch this to default to false (or remove it) so genuine verification can happen?

issues.title: Enable disabled TypeScript ESLint rules and fix violations
issues.body:
Three TypeScript ESLint rules are currently disabled in `react/.eslintrc.js` with a TODO comment to fix them:

```javascript
// TODO: Remove these and fix the warnings
'@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-function-type': 0,
'@typescript-eslint/no-wrapper-object-types': 0,
'@typescript-eslint/no-require-imports': 0
```

### Impact
- **Reduced type safety** from using generic `Function` type instead of specific signatures
- **Potential confusion** from using `String`/`Number` wrapper types instead of primitives
- **Mixed module syntax** with `require()` instead of ES6 imports

### Proposed Solution
1. Run ESLint with these rules enabled to identify all violations
2. Fix each violation across the codebase
3. Remove the rule overrides from `.eslintrc.js`

issues.title: [Bug] Unreachable code branch

labels.name: S: needs info
labels.description: Needs more information to be acted on

issues.body:
# Bug Report: Unreachable code branch in [run_lems_with_eden](cci:1://file:///Users/dhirajrathod/Downloads/gsoc%20final%20repos%20/practical%20/pyNeuroML/pyneuroml/runners.py:621:0-666:19)

## Problem Statement
The function [run_lems_with_eden](cci:1://file:///Users/dhirajrathod/Downloads/gsoc%20final%20repos%20/practical%20/pyNeuroML/pyneuroml/runners.py:621:0-666:19) contains a logical error where a code block is unreachable due to a duplicate condition check. Specifically, there is an `elif` statement that checks the exact same condition as the preceding `if` statement. This results in dead code and potentially indicates a missing feature or unintended behavior in how results are returned when [load_saved_data](cci:1://file:///Users/dhirajrathod/Downloads/gsoc%20final%20repos%20/practical%20/pyNeuroML/pyneuroml/runners.py:1005:0-1239:21) is True.



**The Bug:**
In the code snippet below, `load_saved_data` is checked twice in succession:

```python
    if load_saved_data:
        logger.warning("Event saving is not yet supported in EDEN!!")
        return results, {}
    elif load_saved_data:  # <--- CRITICAL: This branch is unreachable
        return results
    else:
        return True

issues.title: Python 2 syntax in tests/features/base.py causes SyntaxError
issues.body:
# Description
In [brian2/tests/features/base.py](cci:7://file:///Users/dhirajrathod/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/brian2/brian2/tests/features/base.py:0:0-0:0) (line 272), there is Python 2 exception syntax that is invalid in Python 3:

python:
except Exception, ex:



# Impact
Since Brian 2 now requires Python 3.12+ (as per pyproject.toml), this code will raise a SyntaxError if any code path attempts to execute the dynamically generated test code in the results() function.

# Steps to Reproduce
The issue is in a string that gets passed to subprocess.Popen via python -c, so the syntax error will only manifest when running certain feature tests.

issues.title: Missing error handling in worker.js for failed WASM initialization
issues.body:
#Problem :
there is an  issue in [worker.js](cci:7://file:///Users/dhirajrathod/Downloads/gsoc%20final%20repos%20/Brain/brian2wasm/brian2wasm/templates/worker.js:0:0-0:0).

Currently, the `Module()` initialization happens in a Promise chain, but there is no `.catch()` block attached to it. This means if something goes wrong during the WASM loading (like an Out Of Memory error or a 404 on the `.wasm` file), the error gets swallowed by the browser as an "Uncaught (in promise)" exception.

From a user's perspective, the simulation would just hang forever (infinite loading spinner) because the main thread never receives a failure message.


#Solution :
I think we should add a simple `.catch()` block there to forward the error back to the main thread, so the UI can show a proper error message instead of failing silently.

In `modules/xmpp/moderator.js` (lines 110-114), there's a fallback for `window.attachEvent` which was only used by Internet Explorer 11:
Since IE11 reached end-of-life in June 2022, this fallback is dead code that never executes.

### Proposed Fix
 Just use `addEventListener` directly:

issues.title: Security: Passwords Stored in Plaintext Memory
issues.body:
PROBLEM: SSH, AWS, and Google Drive passwords are stored as plaintext strings in Python memory, making them vulnerable to memory dumps, process inspection tools, error logging, and swap files.

CURRENT CODE (Vulnerable):

password = input("Enter SSH password: ")
Password stays in memory with no cleanup


PROPOSED FIX:
1) Use getpass for secure input:

2) Clear password from memory after use:

issues.title: Installation script blocks Python 3.10.17+ due to overly strict version check

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Label to indicate an issue is a regression

issues.body:
## Why this is a problem

Python patch versions (the third number - 3.10.16, 3.10.17, etc.) are designed to be backward-compatible. They mainly contain:

- Security vulnerability fixes
- Bug patches
- Stability improvements

The whole point of patch versions is that you should upgrade to them! By forcing exactly 3.10.16, we're actually preventing developers from using more secure, patched versions of Python.

## We should do

Instead of checking for an exact version, we should check that:

- Major version is 3
- Minor version is 10
- Patch version is at least 16 (or higher)

This way:

- Python 3.10.16 still works (nothing breaks)
- Python 3.10.17, 3.10.18, 3.10.19+ all work (developers can stay secure)
- Python 3.9.x or earlier gets rejected (incompatible)
- Python 3.11+ gets rejected (we're not ready for that yet, which makes sense given #21214)

## Context

The `.python-version` file correctly has `3.10.16` in it, which is perfect - that's the recommended version that pyenv will use. But the installation script should treat that as a minimum, not an exact requirement.

I checked and Python 3.10 is still getting security updates (latest is 3.10.18 or so), so this is pretty relevant for anyone setting up Oppia right now.

## I can fix this!

I'd be happy to submit a PR for this. I'm thinking:

- Update the version check logic
- Add unit tests for different Python versions (3.10.15 fails, 3.10.16 passes, 3.10.17+ passes, 3.11 fails)
- Maybe update the wiki to clarify the version requirements?

## Environment

- **OS:** macOS
- **Python version:** 3.10.17 (installed via pyenv)
- **Got this error running:** `python -m scripts.install_third_party_libs`

## URL of the page where the issue is observed

N/A

## Steps To Reproduce

1. Install Python 3.10.17+
2. Run: `python -m scripts.install_third_party_libs`
3. Error: `'Please use Python 3.10.16'`

## Expected Behavior

Should accept Python 3.10.16+ (any 3.10.x patch version >= 16)

## Screenshots/Videos

No response

## Additional context

- **File:** `scripts/install_third_party_libs.py:100`
- **Issue:** Exact version check blocks security patches
- **Fix:** Change `!=` to range check (allow 3.10.16+)

## Tips for developers

Note: If this is your first Oppia issue, please make sure to follow our guidelines for choosing an issue

issues.title: Update deprecated ESLint rules to their modern equivalents
issues.body:
## Issue
The `.eslintrc.js` file contains 4 deprecated ESLint rules that should be updated to their modern equivalents.

## Deprecated Rules
1. `no-native-reassign` → `no-global-assign`
2. `no-negated-in-lhs` → `no-unsafe-negation`  
3. `indent-legacy` → (remove, use `indent`)
4. `id-blacklist` → `id-denylist`

## Impact
- Prevents future ESLint upgrades
- Shows deprecation warnings in console
- May break in ESLint v9+

## Suggested Fix
Update the rule names in `.eslintrc.js` as per the official ESLint migration guide.

issues.title: [BUG]:scripts.start fails with shell=True when path contains spaces

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Label to indicate an issue is a regression

issues.body:
### Describe the bug

I encountered a bug while trying to set up the Oppia development environment on macOS. The server startup fails completely when the Oppia repository is cloned into a directory path that contains spaces.

This is a real issue that affects new contributors who might naturally clone the repository into folders like "My Projects",

### URL of the page where the issue is observed.

N/A

### Steps To Reproduce



1. Clone the Oppia repository into a directory path containing spaces:
   git clone https://github.com/oppia/oppia.git "/Users/username/my oppia project/oppia"
   
2. Navigate to the repository and run:
   cd "/Users/username/my oppia project/oppia"
   python -m scripts.start


### Expected Behavior



The development server should start successfully regardless of whether the repository path contains spaces

### Screenshots/Videos

_No response_

### What device are you using?

Desktop

### Operating System

MacOS

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Other

### Browser version

_No response_

### Additional context


The issue is in `scripts/servers.py` where several functions use `shell=True` with subprocess commands. When `shell=True` is used, command arguments are joined into a single string before being passed to the shell. However, file paths that contain spaces aren't properly quoted, causing the shell to interpret them as multiple separate arguments.


I have the fixes ready and tested in my local environment. Happy to submit a PR if this issue gets triaged and approved!


### Tips for developers


I discovered this issue while setting up Oppia for the first time . The setup process was otherwise smooth, but this particular issue caused multiple failures that were confusing to debug initially. Fixing this would improve the onboarding experience for new contributors.

issues.title: Backend container crashes on 'make dev' due to missing taxonomy files - macOS
issues.body:
## Problem

When setting up the development environment on macOS using `make dev`, the backend container crashes immediately after starting. The logs show that taxonomy files are missing, but `make dev` does not appear to build them automatically before starting the services.

This creates a confusing first experience for new contributors and waste a lot of time in debugging 

## Environment

- **OS:** macOS
- **Setup method:** Fresh clone, followed by `make dev`
- **Docker:** Docker Desktop for Mac

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Clone the repository
2. Run `make dev`
3. Observe that the command completes, but the backend service is unhealthy or restarting.
4. Check logs: `docker logs po_off-backend-1`

## Actual Behavior

The backend container exits with the following error in the logs:
'''
[perl:error] Could not load taxonomy: /mnt/podata/build-cache/taxonomies-result/units.result at /opt/product-opener/lib/ProductOpener/Tags.pm line 2655.
Compilation failed in require at (eval 2) line 1.
[perl:error] Can't load Perl file: /opt/product-opener/lib/startup_apache2.pl for server openfoodfacts.localhost:0, exiting...
'''

## Expected Behavior

"make dev" should automatically build the required taxonomies (or wait for them) so that the backend starts successfully without manual intervention.

## Workaround

I had to manually run the build scripts inside the container to fix it:

```bash
# Build taxonomies
docker compose run --rm --no-deps backend /opt/product-opener/scripts/taxonomies/build_tags_taxonomy.pl

# Build language files
docker compose run --rm --no-deps backend perl -I/opt/product-opener/lib -I/opt/perl/local/lib/perl5 /opt/product-opener/scripts/build_lang.pl

# Build pro platform fields
docker compose run --rm --no-deps backend /opt/product-opener/scripts/build_pro_platform_fields_columns_names.pl
```

issues.title: Fix browser autocomplete
issues.body: ## What - Fixes browser autocomplete dropdown overlapping with the barcode scanner button in the search bar - Added `autocomplete="off"` attribute to search input fields ## Why When users type in the search bar and browser autocomplete suggestions appear from previous search history, the turquoise barcode scanner button overlaps with the dropdown, creating a visual obstruction and affecting usability. ## Changes - Added `autocomplete="off"` to main navigation search input (line 230) - Added `autocomplete="off"` to mobile navigation search input (line 270) ## Testing 1. Navigate to the homepage 2. Click the search input and type previously searched text 3. Verify no browser autocomplete appears 4. Confirm search functionality still works correctly ## Screenshot

issues.title: Search input field displays overlapping circular button element
issues.body: ## What - When typing text into the "Search for a product" input field at the top of the page, a turquoise/cyan circular button element appears overlapping the input text, creating a visual obstruction. ## Steps to reproduce the behavior 1. Navigate to https://world.openfoodfacts.org/ 2. Click on the "Search for a product" input field at the top of the page 3. Type any text (e.g., "apple") 4. Observe the turquoise circular button appearing over the text ## Expected behavior - The input field should display text clearly without any overlapping elements - Any action buttons (clear/close button) should be properly positioned at the end of the input without obscuring the text - The UI should remain clean and usable ## Actual behavior - A turquoise circular button appears in the middle/end of the input field, overlapping the typed text - This makes the search input less usable and affects the UI/UX ## Additional context This is a UI/UX issue affecting the usability of the search functionality on the home page.

issues.title: Add crawler for RIPE RIS Historical BGP Data
issues.body:
*Overview

Add a crawler to collect historical BGP data from RIPE RIS (Routing Information Service) and integrate it with IHR database.

*Data Source

- **URL**: https://www.ripe.net/analyse/internet-measurements/routing-information-service/
- **Format**: MRT (Multi-threaded Routing Toolkit) binary
- **Access**: Public via HTTP, RIS API, and archive downloads
- **Coverage**: Global BGP collection from ~1000+ peers with real-time + historical data

*New Data Types

- `BGP_UPDATE`: Individual BGP update messages
- `BGP_WITHDRAWAL`: BGP withdrawal messages
- `RIS_COLLECTOR`: Collection points metadata

 *Key Benefits

- Historical BGP route analysis
- AS path analysis and convergence studies
- Internet topology reconstruction
- BGP anomaly detection
- Prefix hijacking research

*Implementation Approach

- Use existing libraries: `pyasn` or `cymru-whois` for BGP processing
- Incremental updates to avoid full data re-import

issues.title: Bug: Fullscreen button displays tooltip/label overlay on preview modal
issues.body: # Description When clicking the fullscreen button, the text label "Fullscreen" appears as an overlay on the preview output modal, which looks like a UI glitch and temporarily obscures content. # Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to https://playground.accordproject.org/ 2. Click the "Fullscreen" button (located on the left sidebar) 3. Observe the preview output modal that open # Responsive Design Issue **CRITICAL**: On smaller/minimized screen sizes, the "Fullscreen" label tooltip becomes extremely problematic: - The tooltip overlays directly on the preview content - The text becomes completely unreadable - Users cannot view the modal content until the label disappears - This makes the fullscreen feature unusable on smaller viewports (tablets, narrow windows) The issue is severity increases significantly on narrow screens where the modal takes up most of the viewport.s # Expected Behavior The preview modal should open cleanly without any tooltip/label overlays obscuring the content. # Actual Behavior The "Fullscreen" text label appears as an overlay on the preview modal, creating a visual glitch that temporarily obscures content. The label disappears after a moment, but it's distracting and unprofessional. # Additional Context This appears to be a CSS/styling issue where the button label tooltip is not being properly hidden when entering fullscreen mode, or it's being rendered on top of the modal instead of being dismissed.

issues.title: Feature Request: Add download options for generated output (TXT, Markdown, HTML) alongside PDF
issues.body:
## Problem

Currently, the Template Playground only supports PDF download. Users who want to:
- Quickly edit the generated text in their preferred editor
- Version control the output (TXT/MD work better with Git)
- Integrate generated content with other tools
- Work without requiring a PDF reader

...must manually copy-paste the preview text or convert the PDF to another format.

# Proposed Solution

Add download options for multiple text-based formats alongside the existing PDF download:

1. **Plain Text (.txt)** — Simple, raw text export without any formatting
2. **Markdown (.md)** — Preserves basic text structure, easily editable, great for version control
3. **HTML (.html)** — Optional; for users who want formatted web-ready output

## Benefits

* Better for users who need to edit and modify the output
* Supports different workflows (version control, text editors, automation)
* Complements (not replaces) the existing PDF option
* Reuses already-rendered output — likely just needs format conversion
* Improves user experience for diverse use cases

# Implementation Approach

- Add a **format selector dropdown** or **button group** next to the existing "Download PDF" button
- Reuse the existing rendered text/HTML output
- Trigger browser download with appropriate MIME type and file extension
- Start with TXT and MD; HTML can be added later if needed

# Current Status

This issue is currently in the discussion phase.
No pull request has been opened yet — this proposal is intended to gather feedback and align with maintainers before implementation.

issues.body: ### Describe the bug The header image in the blog post “Between JSON Schema and Relational ### Steps To Reproduce 1) Open the blog post on a mobile device 2) Scroll to the header image 3) Observe the image layout 4) Notice the oversized black margins and incorrect cropping ### Expected Behavior * The blog header image should: * Fit properly within its container * Not show large empty black areas * Maintain correct aspect ratio * Display the JSON Schema + Database icons fully and clearly ### Screenshots ### Device Information [optional] ```markdown - OS: - Browser: - version: ``` ### Are you working on this issue? Yes ### Do you think this work might require an [Architectural Decision Record (ADR)]? (significant or noteworthy) No
